data.title: MRC Global Launches $125 Million Share Buyback Plan
data.description:
Don't Miss Our New Year's Offers:

  • Discover the latest stocks recommended by top Wall Street analysts, all in one place with Analyst Top Stocks
  • Make smarter investments with weekly expert stock picks from the Smart Investor Newsletter

MRC Global ( (MRC) ) just unveiled an update.

MRC Global has announced a $125 million share repurchase program authorized by its Board of Directors, set to expire in January 2028. This move is part of their capital allocation strategy aimed at maintaining a net debt leverage ratio below 1.5x, returning cash to shareholders, and investing in future growth, reflecting confidence in their financial position and operational efficiency.

More about MRC Global

Headquartered in Houston, Texas, MRC Global is a leading global distributor of pipe, valves, fittings, and other infrastructure products and services, catering to sectors such as gas utilities, downstream, industrial and energy transition, and production and transmission infrastructure. With over a century of experience, the company offers innovative supply chain solutions, technical expertise, and a digital platform from a network of over 200 locations, simplifying the supply chain for approximately 10,000 customers.

Average Trading Volume: 522,662

Technical Sentiment Consensus Rating: Sell

Current Market Cap: $1.08B

data.title: Nokia Initiates Share Buyback Program to Counteract Dilution
data.description:
Don't Miss Our New Year's Offers:

  • Discover the latest stocks recommended by top Wall Street analysts, all in one place with Analyst Top Stocks
  • Make smarter investments with weekly expert stock picks from the Smart Investor Newsletter

Nokia ( (GB:0HAF) ) has provided an announcement.

Nokia Corporation has initiated a share buyback program to mitigate the dilution caused by the issuance of new shares to Infinera Corporation shareholders and related incentives. The program, authorized by Nokia’s Annual General Meeting and compliant with EU regulations, aims to repurchase 150 million shares for up to EUR 900 million by the end of 2025. On January 3, 2025, Nokia repurchased 872,093 shares at an average price of EUR 4.31 per share, amounting to a total transaction cost of EUR 3,762,907, increasing its treasury shares to 222,986,522.

More about Nokia

Nokia is a B2B technology innovation leader, known for pioneering networks that sense, think, and act. The company operates across mobile, fixed, and cloud networks, creating value through intellectual property and long-term research, particularly via Nokia Bell Labs. Nokia’s high-performance networks integrate seamlessly into any ecosystem, offering opportunities for monetization and scale, trusted by service providers, enterprises, and partners worldwide.

YTD Price Performance: 1.41%

Average Trading Volume: 2,546,016

Technical Sentiment Consensus Rating: Sell

Current Market Cap: €23.52B

Learn more about 0HAF stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Mynaric AG Revises 2024 Financial Guidance Amid Shipment Delays
data.description:
Don't Miss Our New Year's Offers:

  • Discover the latest stocks recommended by top Wall Street analysts, all in one place with Analyst Top Stocks
  • Make smarter investments with weekly expert stock picks from the Smart Investor Newsletter

An update from Mynaric AG ( (DE:M0YN) ) is now available.

Mynaric AG has updated its 2024 financial guidance, lowering its revenue forecast due to delays in shipping CONDOR Mk3 terminals, with expected revenue now at EUR 14.1 million, down from a previous estimate of EUR 16.0 to EUR 24.0 million. This adjustment is attributed to component shortages causing shipment delays into 2025, impacting the company’s optical communications terminal backlog, now projected at 787 units instead of the anticipated 800 to 1,000 units. The company maintains its forecast for operating loss and cash-in from customer contracts, reflecting a stable outlook amid these logistical challenges.

More about Mynaric AG

Mynaric is a leader in laser communications, providing optical communications terminals for air, space, and mobile applications. The company focuses on ultra-high data rates and secure, long-distance data transmission, serving wireless terrestrial, mobility, airborne, and space-based sectors. With headquarters in Munich, Mynaric also operates in Los Angeles and Washington, D.C.

YTD Price Performance: -0.14%

Average Trading Volume: 165

Technical Sentiment Consensus Rating: Buy

Current Market Cap: $21.12M

First Bancorp ( (FNLC) ) has provided an update.

The First Bancorp has appointed Christopher J. Austin as Executive Vice President and Chief Legal Counsel for First National Bank. Austin, who brings 26 years of experience in real estate, commercial lending, and corporate law, is expected to enhance the bank’s leadership team and contribute to its operational strategy.

More about First Bancorp

First National Bank, a subsidiary of The First Bancorp, Inc., is an independent community bank with 18 offices across several counties in Maine. The bank focuses on providing financial services within its local communities.

data.title: Franklin Wireless Announces Executive Leadership Changes
data.description:
Don't Miss Our New Year's Offers:

  • Discover the latest stocks recommended by top Wall Street analysts, all in one place with Analyst Top Stocks
  • Make smarter investments with weekly expert stock picks from the Smart Investor Newsletter

Franklin Wireless ( (FKWL) ) has issued an announcement.

Effective January 1, 2025, Bill Bauer will resign as acting Chief Financial Officer of Franklin Wireless but will continue as General Counsel and Director of Strategic Planning. Reid Granados has been appointed as Interim CFO, bringing over 20 years of finance and executive management experience, potentially enhancing Franklin Wireless’s financial strategies and operations.
